epigraph.text: A threefold cord is not quickly broken.

content.text:
A triangle is formed by three noncollinear points and the segments 
connecting them. Despite this simplicity, triangles exhibit rich variety 
and deep properties that form the foundation of geometric reasoning.

content.title: Triangle
content.content:
A **triangle** is a figure formed by three segments 
joining three noncollinear points. The three points are called 
**vertices**; the segments are called **sides**.

content.title: Triangle Classification by Sides
content.content:
- **Scalene triangle**: No two sides 
 are congruent.

- **Isosceles triangle**: At least 
 two sides are congruent.

- **Equilateral triangle**: All 
 three sides are congruent.

content.title: Triangle Classification by Angles
content.content:
- **Acute triangle**: All three angles 
 are acute (less than $90°$).

- **Right triangle**: One angle is a 
 right angle ($90°$).

- **Obtuse triangle**: One angle is 
 obtuse (greater than $90°$).

content.title: Triangle Sum Theorem
content.content: The sum of the measures of the interior angles of a triangle is $180°$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Corollary: Acute Angles in a Right Triangle
content.content: The acute angles of a right triangle are complementary.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Exterior Angle Theorem
content.content:
The measure of an exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the 
measures of the two remote interior angles.
content.label: Theorem

marginNotes.text:
By wisdom a house is built, and by 
understanding it is established.
